title: pittosporum ellipticum var. ovatum
intro: Pittosporum ellipticum var. ovatum, also known as the Tāheke or Pittosporum, is a small, evergreen tree native to New Zealand. The medicinal properties of this plant have been utilized by the indigenous people, who use its bark and leaves to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Tāheke include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Traditional herbal preparations include making a tea from the leaves and bark, which can be used to treat various ailments, as well as using the plant in topical applications to soothe skin irritations.
